programme.desc: Vanessa is suing her former roommates for kicking her out and leaving her homeless.

programme.title: The People's Court
programme.desc: Robert says he loaned the defendant money and she agreed to pay $100 a month in late fees until the loan was paid off, but took 43 extra months to pay it.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Two moms ask Dr. Phil to give their sons a reality check. Rita says her son, Eric, changed his name to “King Keith” and is forgoing a college education to pursue a singing career. Has he lost touch with reality? And, Cheryl says her college-educated son, Patrick, is lazy and lacks ambition.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: TV Star Nicholas Brendon has been starring in his own private alcohol-fueled drama which has led to multiple arrests and been documented in celebrity magazines, social media and blogs. Nicholas sits down with Dr. Phil -- and abruptly walks offstage. Is Nicholas ready to take the steps needed to save his life and career?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.sub-title: Bad Boy Blake with Abs of Steel
programme.desc: Blake, 26, abandons his education to smoke pot all day in his mother's rent-free house, where he is verbally abusive and refuses to work.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Amanda Knox spent four years locked in an Italian prison for the murder of her British roommate Meredith Kercher. Dubbed “Foxy Knoxy” by the press, she became one of the most famous cases of wrongful conviction. She tells her story, as she fights to save the life of Texas death row inmate Rodney Reed. This program contains graphic discussion about a criminal case. Viewer discretion advised.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Terrell Owens is a six-time NFL pro-bowler and arguably one of the best wide receivers to play the game, but he may be better known for his antics off the field. His tumultuous career has been well-documented, and he's been called arrogant, toxic and friendless. The football great sits down with Dr. Phil to set the record straight about the bad rap he says he’s been getting, especially from the mothers of three of his children, who claim he's also a deadbeat dad who is behind on child support payments and makes little attempt to see his kids. What does Terrell think of their accusations? See what happens when the moms come together to confront the alleged absentee dad on Dr. Phil's stage about missed birthday parties, ignored phone calls and empty promises. Is Terrell living in the lap of luxury while his kids go without? What kind of relationship does he want with his children? Dr. Phil delves into Terrell's past to help him create a better future for his children. Plus, at 38, will he play in the NFL again?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.sub-title: I Hate My Daughter In Law
programme.desc: Family conflict between a woman and her daghter-in-law.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.sub-title: Mom: Let Us Speak to Our Dying Dad!
programme.desc: Four adult siblings say they have been blacklisted by their mother from seeing their dying father.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Some people are just unusual. But today unusual means shocking and embarrassing! Dr. Phil’s guest want to know if their eccentric loved ones are worth dealing with. The first guest shops so much she’s stockpiled herself right out of her own home. Her eighty-year-old mother has to deliver newspapers to help pay off her mounting debts! Then—another guest is dating a plumber who likes to wear women’s clothes. She doesn’t know whether to leave him or marry him. Dr. Phil has some straight words for this odd couple.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: You may know that one in four women will become a victim of domestic violence, but did you know that one in nine men will too? Violence against men is rarely talked about and under-reported. Dr. Phil continues his campaign to end the silence on domestic violence with two couples willing to open up about their abusive relationships. Steve says his wife, Monica, punches him and recently pushed him down a flight of stairs. He says he’s not only afraid of her abuse, he’s afraid of how he may retaliate in the heat of anger. The couple has nine children between them – two of whom are severely disabled – and they’re worried about the effect their explosive arguments have on their kids. Then, Charlie worries she’ll get so angry at her new husband, Gary, that she’ll lash out and inadvertently kill him. Gary says that everything he does makes his wife mad, and he just can’t win. Can these women learn to control their tempers and stop the chaos before something tragic happens? Learn more about Dr. Phil’s End the Silence on Domestic Violence campaign and pledge to become a silence breaker!

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: What do 22-year-old “van life” blogger Gabby Petito’s final videos reveal about what might have happened to her? Gabby and her fiancé, Brian Laundrie, recorded themselves traveling cross country, but two months after leaving Florida, Brian returned to Florida alone. He refused to speak to investigators and her family -- and is now missing. Plus, despite posting videos looking like a happy couple, police released a very different kind of video -- bodycam video of a distraught Gabby crying about an altercation with Brian in the days before she disappeared. TV host Nancy Grace, a former law enforcement officer and FBI defense tactics instructor, a criminal defense attorney, and a body language expert, all weigh in on the unusual circumstances.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Do you have a nosy loved one in your life snooping through your things, questioning your every move and driving you crazy? Dr. Phil has advice.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: When you get married, you look forward to a life filled with love, happiness and security. But what if you find out your spouse has been telling you lies and is someone completely different than the person you thought you knew?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Children accuse their dad of neglect.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Do you ever feel nervous before posting on social media? Have you ever censored your own thoughts because you worry that you could be criticized or even canceled for posting your beliefs? Dr. Phil and his guests discuss the self-censorship debate on the internet and social media, including the fundamental misunderstanding about the true extent of “freedom of speech” under the First Amendment. Kimberly, a University of Tennessee doctoral candidate, claims her college violated her freedom of speech after a professional conduct committee determined that she had posted unprofessional content to her social media. What rights do colleges have to spy on personal social media posts? Also Tehran Von Ghasri, a comedian who performs on college campuses, says cancel culture gone too far. How does the fear of being canceled affects the jokes he performs for college students or content he posts online? Alexandra, a social media influencer and business owner, says giving opinions about COVID got her censored and three accounts closed. What did she say to get canceled? Dr. Phil also talks with Attorney Robert Shibley, a Senior Fellow at the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression (FIRE), who says over half of Americans are not saying how they feel for fear of offending others or being canceled. Jessica Levinson, an attorney and a Clinical Professor of Law at Loyola Law School, provides an explanation on the boundaries of the First Amendment. And, Gabrielle Gambrell, a Columbia University and NYU professor, says that social media sites are obligated to monitor hate and dangerous speech. Plus, find out which emojis you are probably using right now are deemed outdated or inappropriate!

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Natalie says her husband, Brian, was in a tragic work accident in 2015 when he fell from a 68-foot crane. She says he was paralyzed from the knees down and claims he has stopped trying to live life. Natalie claims that since the accident, Brian has treated her and their 13-year old son, Jacob, like his slaves instead of his family. Natalie says her marriage with Brian has changed, and they don’t know how to communicate with one another. Brian says his family doesn’t understand what he is going through and that he is in too much pain to move. Brian also says Natalie’s parents, Sharyn and Dave, are too involved in his family’s lives, which is causing tension. Sharyn and Dave say they were trying to help their daughter since Natalie took on all of the responsibilities in her household.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Lisa says her four beautiful adult daughters, Brandi, Wendi, Laci and Tahni, are "monsters" who pick on her, and their constant drama is tearing apart their family. However, all four daughters say it's Lisa who needs help. They claim she's a "textbook narcissist" who constantly plays the victim and has always put men before her family. Who is in the wrong here? Will Dr. Phil be able to bring this family back together?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: When Pierre, his wife, Amanda, and his mother, Yolande, came to see Dr. Phil, the couple complained his mom was a meddling-mother-in-law. Amanda said Yolande never approved of her, and Yolande admitted she wanted her son to be with a more educated woman. Is this family still at odds?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Jared says his ex-wife, Chandra, is a deadbeat mom who has failed to pay her court-ordered child support in the last six months. He also accuses Chandra’s current husband, Jason, of physical abuse toward his two children when they’re in his care -- a claim Jason adamantly denies. Chandra accuses Jared of emotionally abusing her since their divorce five years ago, and she claims he’s determined to ruin her life with her new husband. Is Jared a concerned parent, or a bitter ex with an ulterior motive? And, with their children caught in the crossfire, can these exes stop pointing fingers and learn how to peacefully co-parent? Plus, why hasn’t Chandra paid child support? Then, don’t miss Dr. Phil’s tips on how to effectively co-parent with your ex!

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Deborah was devastated when she went through her 17-year-old daughter, Miranda’s, cell phone and discovered a very disturbing photo. Miranda says she tried to tell her mom about her pregnancy but couldn’t bring herself to do it. Learn why Deborah says she was forced to turn her daughter in to the police, and how Miranda ended up in jail. Dr. Phil reaches out to the teen struggling to deal with guilt and loss, who faces backlash from a community she says doesn’t understand. Then, a family is torn apart after the father confesses the secret he’s kept for years. Find out why his girlfriend is now afraid for their 2-year-old daughter.

programme.title: Dr.Phil
programme.desc: What happened to the victims of self-proclaimed FLDS prophet, Warren Jeffs?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: A former FLDS spokesperson, Willie Jessop, comes face to face with critics.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: It's a mystery that has America absolutely baffled. An Idaho teenager and her younger brother with special needs vanish and have not been seen by anyone in almost four months. Their mother, Lori Vallow, appears completely unbothered by the fact that her two children are just gone, and is in Hawaii seemingly carefree with her fifth and newest husband, Chad Daybell, who reportedly has extreme religious beliefs. Adding to the mystery, four untimely deaths surround Lori and Chad, yet she still refuses to say a single word about her children’s whereabouts. How could these children just go missing for months without a trace? Where are they? Today, family members of Lori Vallow speak to Dr. Phil to try and shine a light on this unbelievable situation. Don’t miss it!

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: In an exclusive interview, 16-year-old twins Georgia and Walker “Patterson” Inman, who are reportedly set to inherit $1 billion as the only living heirs of the Duke tobacco fortune, open up to Dr. Phil about the abuse they claim they suffered for more than a decade at the hands of their now-deceased father, Walker Inman Jr. Hear why they say their lives have been anything but privileged -- and why they lived every day in a state of pure terror.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Dr. Phil continues his exclusive interview with 16-year-old twins Georgia and Patterson Inman, heirs to the Duke tobacco fortune and speaks to their mother, Daisha, who is battling the bank for control of her children’s trust funds. How does Daisha answer to allegations that she is trying to drain the twins’ accounts? Plus, how can the children move forward and begin to heal after the years of abuse they say they suffered at the hands of their late father?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: This is a true crime saga like no other. The Murdaugh family is one of the most prominent families in South Carolina and has served in powerful legal positions dating back nearly 100 years. Rumors swirled that Alex Murdaugh and his family could get away with anything. They have been tied to the mysterious deaths of their housekeeper, a high school classmate, and a young woman in a fatal boat crash. In June 2021, Murdaugh allegedly found his wife and younger son shot dead on their hunting lodge property. About a year later, Murdaugh was charged for their murders. The killings set off a bizarre chain of events that included Murdaugh staging his own murder. There are also nearly 100 financial crimes that are linked to him. Dr. Phil is joined by a panel of experts: former criminal prosecutor Loni Coombs; Michal Higdon, anchor and investigative reporter at WCSC-TV Charleston; forensic expert Joseph Scott Morgan; and former FBI agent Bobby Chacon to discuss this case.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.sub-title: Post Malone: Love Story or Scam?
programme.desc: Two women believe their grandmother is being scammed by an unknown online imposter posing as rapper Post Malone.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: In-laws can be the kind who give unwanted advice, stop by unexpectedly and use guilt and manipulation to get what they want. But sometimes, they are loving parents who get caught in the middle of their child's relationship. Amanda calls her mother-in-law, Yolande, psychotic, controlling and meddling. She says Yolande speaks to her son, Pierre, in French so she can hide the hurtful words she’s using about Amanda. Yolande says Amanda is a drunk, and an uneducated woman who is wrecking her son's life. Why does Pierre say he can't choose a side? To test Amanda's theory of why Yolande and Pierre speak French to each other, Dr. Phil sets up a "special" dinner for the three of them. Be a fly on the wall, and find out if Amanda's right or just being paranoid. And, John and Chrissy's engagement is on hold because they say they can't stop fighting. They hit and slap each other, and call each other vulgar names — often in front of their 8-month-old son. John's mom, Charlene, who is often pulled into their fights, says they both need to grow up! After watching themselves scream and yell on tape, will this couple decide to drop their fists, close their mouths and work to make a peaceful home for their baby?

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: The case of Thomas Cogdell, who confessed to his sister's muder at age 12, is examined.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Sarah is a pretty, intelligent, successful woman with everything going for her — so why won't she leave her abusive boyfriend, Robert? She says he punches her, chokes her, spits in her face, and has even threatened to kill her, but she believes he can be rehabilitated. Is she in over her head? If you or someone you know is a victim of domestic violence, don’t miss Dr. Phil's exit action plan for safely leaving an abusive partner

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: A man wants Dr. Phil's help in deciding between his wife of 8 years or girlfriend of 2 years.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: A husband cheated on his wife with her sister.

programme.title: Dr. Phil
programme.desc: Revisiting the 1992 murder of Shanda Sharer, her family reflects on coping and forgiveness.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A man outfitted in just his underwear is taken into custody. A dad gets busted for DUI while on his way to pick up a pizza for his kids. Officers recover drugs from a suspect’s pocket who is puzzled as to how they got there. A woman learns the hard way “what happens in Vegas stays in Vegas”.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A man in detention is paranoid about officers handling him inappropriately. A man in custody claims to have a seizure. A man is taken in to custody for stealing videogaming equipment.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A disorderly man shouts to the heavens and asks for divine intervention. Twin brothers are arrested after a fray with police. A man in women’s clothing is booked after harassing passengers on a bus.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: In San Diego, CA an officer grapples with a combative detainee. In Portland, OR six party goers are taken into custody. In Fort Worth, TX a high-risk inmate manages to get the key to his handcuffs.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: In Reno, NV an intoxicated man is placed in isolation after exhibiting combative behavior. In Portland, OR a man wearing a red dress is brought in for trespassing and tells officers that he is in the FBI. In Reno, NV two young men are set straight after stealing a car.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: An intoxicated man claims to be connected to the occupy Wall Street movement. Officers struggle with a combative man, and a tearful candy thief is taken into custody.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A hysterical woman is transferred to an isolation cell. A woman shows her undying LA pride to the arresting officers. A young woman is put under observation for threatening harm to herself.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Cousins come to jail together but only one leaves. Officers contend the best they can with an unruly inmate. Naked meditation in a parking lot lands a street artist in jail.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A man admits to urinating in public. A couple spends their date night in jail after being over served, and a DUI suspect passes out during booking.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: An inmate goes on a rampage when he suspects police of losing his money. Officers have a tough time with a drunk man. An alleged prostitute is stabbed. A drunk trapeze artist takes a drive on his moped.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: An uncooperative woman is placed in a restraint chair. A troubled woman with a drug addiction is brought in on prostitution charges. One man is taken to a sobering cell to sleep off his intoxication.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Code Blue alarms sound as inmates start fighting inside the men’s jail. An irate man with a huge beard refuses to remove bands inside his facial hair. A man with deep stab wounds stumbles into jail.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Officers try to deal with a sloppy and combative drunk. A man gets locked up after he blows all his casino winnings, and a woman gets tricked by her bail bondsman.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: In Sarasota, FL officers place a hostile former Marine in isolation. In Portland, OR officers take a man into custody for joyriding in a stolen car. In Las Vegas, NV a jailed man is more concerned about the condition of his car rather than the people involved in the accident that was hospitalized.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Acts of aggression cause a man to be placed in a restraint chair. Jail lockdown occurs after an inmate is stabbed with a pencil. After being booked for road rage a woman unleashes a venomous tirade.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A woman is brought in for attacking her boyfriend. A talkative inmate is moved to isolation, and an daring inmate attempts to escape his restraints.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: In Salt Lake City, UT a disorderly man in the detention center is sent to the padded room. A Sarasota, FL man hurls insults at officers while in custody. In Portland, OR a drunk and destructive young man is placed in the drunk-tank. In Las Vegas, NV a woman tries to lie about the reasons behind her detention.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A dominatrix is arrested for playing rough. A father is accused of assaulting his daughter's boyfriend, and an inmate's negative attitude leads to trouble.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A passenger allegedly attacked a cabdriver. A woman describes assaulting her boyfriend with hot food, and a mother is arrested for leaving her kids in a car while gambling.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: Two sisters go on a mini-crime spree, get caught and find no one will bail them out. A domestic argument about night fishing lands a husband in jail for breaking into his own car. An irate inmate blows off steam by doing laps in her jail cell. A woman finds out it’s hard to stop selling drugs when the neighborhood knows her business.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A code red call blares after a fight occurs in the jail’s dorms. Accused of trespassing, a woman removes her clothes from her holding cell. A man is brought into detox after slamming against a cop car.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: In Las Vegas, NV an intoxicated man is strapped to a restraining chair. In Portland, OR a seemingly gracious man is arrested for assault. programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: A woman is brought into the detention center hogtied. A disruptive teen is sent to a holding cell. Casino valets tip the authorities about an intoxicated woman leaving behind the wheel.

programme.title: Jail
programme.desc: In Las Vegas, NV a man who insists he paid for a speeding ticket becomes so incensed that he is placed in a restraining chair. In Sarasota, FL a belligerent man accuses officers of touching him inappropriately. In Portland, OR officers help a young mother and her kids.

programme.title: Jail

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: A 1972 Dodge Charger is restored.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.sub-title: Drive a Mile in My Shoes
programme.desc: A 1969 Road Runner is featured.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: Work on a 1972 Dodge Charger is continued.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: The story about the 1972 Charger is briefly revisited, and past work leading to its completion is also revisited. Work on the 1972 Dodge Charger is completed and revealed to the owners.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: The 1970 Plymouth Superbird work is resumed, and the 1970 Charger R/T collision repair is completed. The 1970 Barracuda Convertible's 383 2bbl. V8 is assembled and painted.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: A secret is accidentally revealed by Mark. Mark and Daren visit the local AAMCO Transmissions to watch the overhaul of the 1970 Barracuda Convertible's 727 Automatic Transmission, and then the Barracuda has its A/C box and instrument panel installed. The 1970 Superbird assembly nears completion.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: Automotive parts supplier Tony D'Agostino of Tony's Parts helps the shop get organized, and Mark tests his Mopar knowledge against Tony's. Work on a 1970 Plymouth Superbird is completed.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.sub-title: The Hills Have Eyes on a Superbird
programme.desc: The 1970 Superbird's restoration and story is recapped, and the Hills visit the shop to take delivery of the car, along with its original owner. Mike and Michael Hill drive the Superbird on a cross county trip home to South Carolina.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: With the 1970 Plymouth Superbird done and safely driven home, Mark is asked to restore a 1969 Daytona Charger. Due to its condition, Mark questions if it's practical and if it's even legal.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: Just when things couldn't get more stressful, Mark agrees to finally get the 1930 Chevy Coach fully restored by the end of the week. Meanwhile, Mark QC's the next cars in the process of resurrection.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: See the complete 3-year restoration of Kimberly Cook's 1970 Barracuda Convertible: the body, paint, engine, and convertible top, culminating in Graveyard Carz' most emotional reveal to date.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: New shop. New crew. An All-new Graveyard Carz! The ghouls tackle a 1970 Cuda.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: Mark paints a Hemi Charger in his favorite Mopar color, burnt orange. AMD delivers the Phantom Cuda, and the ghouls finish the Dailey's 1970 Cuda 383, a project four years in the making.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: The ghouls wrap up the 340 Cuda and install panels on the Hemi Charger. Mark trains Allysa on O.E.M. assembly line markings. The legendary red Plymouth Fury "Christine" visits the shop.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: The ghouls have problems with after-market parts for the 400 Magnum V8. Allysa begins working in the shop, and two rare Mopars are disassembled

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: Mark's coffee addiction is spotlighted, and the ghouls work on a 1970 Challenger.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: Work on a 1970 Cuda 340 is featured.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.sub-title: Hemi Orange is the New Corporate Blue

programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: Mark teaches Allysa to analyze and document cars in preparation for disassembly, but she later has a crucial lapse in judgement. The ghouls install the 400 Magnum in the 1972 Dodge Charger.

programme.desc: E26- After learning the truth as to where the Olivine compound was hidden, Cameron chooses to escape into exile, that is until a confrontation with General Nexxus forces Cameron to realize his true importance to the future of Humanity.

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: S8:E13 Vivian's health is on the line. Kauffman is interrogated by BCPD. Dominique Wagner is named the new CEO at Bay University Hospital. Sara and Lex prepare for their wedding day. Caleb makes a decision.

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: S8:E14 Sara and Lex share their vows. Caleb and RJ’s plans go awry. Adam surprises Daniel. Dominque makes a sinister move. Pete's life is turned upside down.

programme.title: Popstar! Today
programme.desc: S4:E19 Breaking Down Walt Disney Studios, Top 3: Disney Live Action Movies, Report: Pinocchio, Special: Gen Z Celebs Who Started Their Careers On Disney Channel, Profile: Ariana Greenblatt

programme.title: Popstar! Today
programme.desc: S1:E1 Top 3 Hollywood Moms, Kristen Bell, Celebrity Kids Trying To Make It , Stay Healthy While Pregnant, Stars Who’ve Adopted Multiple Kids.

programme.title: Popstar! Today
programme.desc: S1:E2 Models Who Also Act, Not Your Ordinary Supermodels, What to wear when you’re tall, Naomi Campbell

programme.title: Popstar! Today
programme.desc: S1:E3 Best Met Gala Looks, Lady Gaga, Zendaya, Celebs Who are Make Up Gurus, Lewis Hamilton fashion designs.

programme.title: Popstar! Today
programme.desc: S1:E4 Stars Who Dropped a Lot of Weight, Body Positivity, Embrace Your Curves,The Importance of a Personal Trainer, Celebs Gaining and Losing Weight.

programme.title: Popstar! Today
programme.desc: S1:E5 Actresses in Green, Fashion on the Red Carpet , Celebs Who Care About the Environment, How to Eat Vegan, Gwyneth Paltrow, Celebrity Lifestyle Gurus

programme.title: B-Sides with Pete Mar
programme.desc: S10:E36 Maisie Peters talks about her debut album, an acoustic performance from spill tab, and Thomas Headon who chats up his new EP "Victoria".

programme.title: B-Sides with Pete Mar
programme.desc: S10:E37 Pete Mar shares his “B-Sides New Music In Bloom” special featuring Milky Chance, Mt. Joy, Lizzy McAlpine, Sunsleeper, Romero, Magdalena Bay and much more!

programme.title: B-Sides with Pete Mar
programme.desc: S11:E1 Indie artist Aurora talks about her latest "the gods we trust", a stripped-down performance of "Victoria" from Thomas Haden, emerging artist Clay chats up her new ep "Breathing Into Bloom"

programme.title: B-Sides with Pete Mar
programme.desc: S11:E2 Pete Mar attends Lightning in a Bottle 2022. Guest include SOHMI, Annie Hamilton who performs her new track "Whirlwind". Plus, Pete showcases highlights around the festival.

programme.title: Laura McKenzie's Traveler: Christmas Around the World
programme.desc: S18:E9 Laura travels the world in search of Christmas and how many countries celebrate the holiday season.

programme.title: Elizabeth Stanton's Great Big World
programme.desc: S10:E9 Elizabeth and Jennifer Veal enjoy their adventure through history.

programme.title: Lost and Found Music Studios
programme.desc: S2:E2 Leia's plan to fix Luke's van backfires, jeopardizing Rachel and Leia's friendship. Eva breaks her curfew to attend a tailgate party. Michelle reaches out to John, and Leia finally reveals her feelings to Luke.

programme.desc: Euronews è l'unico canale internazionale con una prospettiva davvero europea. La nostra missione è quella di permettere a ciascuno di farsi una propria opinione presentando, con imparzialità, tutti i diversi punti di vista.

programme.title: Roswell Conspiracies: Aliens, Myths and Legends
programme.desc: An alien satellite that has been “watching” us for fifty years collides with a NASA shuttle. Logan hopes the information might reveal information about his father.

programme.title: Roswell Conspiracies: Aliens, Myths and Legends
programme.desc: Cattle mutilation draws Logan to a small town and saddles him with a new partner, Dr. Petrovic. They discover science has spawned more trouble – hybrid, mutant, alien fire ants. Now they must rescue the townsfolk and the Detail Team before Rinaker calls an air strike on the town.

programme.title: Roswell Conspiracies: Aliens, Myths and Legends

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.sub-title: Super Special Sonic Search and Smash Squad
programme.desc: When Sonic and Tails are captured by Grounder and Scratch, Sonic tricks them into letting them escape by reminiscing about how they all met. Several badniks seen early on in this episode later resurfaced in Dr. Robotnik's Mean Bean Machine.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: While on pursuit with Scratch and Grounder, Sonic and Tails hide in an underground mine which a miser mole think that they are trespassers.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic saves a female hedgehog from Scratch and Grounder which Sonic falls in love with, while Dr. Robotnik plans to flood a village by stealing the water from the Mobius reservoir.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic saves a female hedgehog from Scratch and Grounder which Sonic falls in love with, while Dr. Robotnik plans to flood a village by stealing the water from the Mobius reservoir.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic must save a family of sloths from Dr. Robotnik's slow-motion ray, but when he himself is slowed down by Scratch and Grounder, Tails and the sloth family must take action.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Desperate to get workers to build a Robotnik statue, Dr. Robotnik opens Casino Night Zone with his pal Smiley but when Sonic stops Scratch and Grounder, Robotnik traps Tails so Sonic has to make a choice to save his pal.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik imprisons a satirical cartoonist named Sketch Lampoon for making fun of him in his comic book, much to the anger of Sonic, his biggest fan. Sonic purposely allows his own capture as well, but does not expect to be placed in a maximum-security jail cell. With Robotnik angrily sends Sonic in the cell for interfering with his plans once again.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Scratch, Grounder and Coconuts set up a chili-dog trap which then resulted with Tails been captured by a mysterious person so Sonic has to find Tails and for Robotnik and co to capture Sonic.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.sub-title: Close Encounters of the Sonic Kind
programme.desc: While Scratch and Grounder try to catch Sonic using a Suction plate, two aliens, one being a prince, crash lands on Mobius. Sonic meets the prince when he finds out that the prince's baby brother would blow up Mobius which Sonic must save.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik's momma escapes from the home for mothers of villains and demands her birthday present! When she endangers Sonic and Tails, and they manage to have her taken back to the home, even Robotnik is glad to see her go.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Coconuts is exiled by Robotnik to a remote jungle, where he uses a giant baby ape to destroy villages. But Sonic exposes Coconuts as a robot, and the baby ape's real Dad takes Coconuts and the baby ape away for a spanking.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: A country and western singer released a song about Sonic which is making Dr. Robotnik mad and builds a music destroyer robot.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: An energetic salesman named Wes Weasley sells Robotnik numerous gadgets to help Scratch and Grounder capture Sonic. However, Robotnik later comes to regret it when Weaseley's gadgets keep backfiring.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic and Tails rescue Lucas, Robotnik's first prisoner, and attempt to reunite him with his girlfriend, Lucinda. However, Robotnik is also in love with Lucinda, and pursues Lucas.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: En route to stop a train of explosives from arriving at Robotnik's munition factory, Sonic and Tails must deal with a duo of bears named Big Griz and Big Mike after Scratch and Grounder tell them that Sonic and Tails are the enemies.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik takes Professor Von Schlemmer hostage and uses his growth machine to try and enlarge himself. However, the machine backfires, and enlarges Tails instead in nearby Wienerville. Sonic must keep the famished Tails from getting into more danger as Robotnik tricks the citizens into thinking that Sonic is the villain, while Robotnik is the hero planning to save the city.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Sonic decides to find Tails a suitable foster home to ensure his safety, Robotnik finds out and creates a robot fox family, who pose as Tails' long-lost parents.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When overweight, elderly superhero Captain Rescue bungles in his attempts to help Sonic, he grows tired of his incompetence. Robotnik then uses Captain Rescue to successfully imprison Sonic in an impenetrable force field, and Tails and Captain Rescue must save him.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Scratch suffers a blow to the head and believes that he is his favorite television character, Edgar Eagle. Sonic uses this to his advantage in preventing another of Robotnik's schemes.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: There is a Sonic Appreciation Day happening where Sonic is the guest in a hotel. However, someone steals his power sneakers and now Sonic and Tails have to find the culprit.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Tails adopts a cute and fuzzy pet named Walter who turns out to be a black hole who sucks up everything in Mobius. Realizing that Walter is from another dimension our heroes have to return him and they soon find themselves spinning through interdimensional limbo.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Robotnik opens up a resort to make the resident Mobians his slaves, Sonic enlists the help of some robots from an area of Mobius called Scrap Valley.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik creates a robot Sonic suit with the intent of ruining the hedgehog's reputation. He forces a lab rat named Laurence to pilot by threatening his parents.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Dr. Robotnik creates a genius chip so he would have enough brains to rule Mobius, however a computer hacker got hold of it which then the chip landed inside Grounder causing him to be smart.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic leaves Tails in charge of protecting a squirrel village food supply, when he zips off to stop a dam from breaking. Robotnik sends Scratch and Grounder after Tails, who defends himself the best he can with Home Alone booby traps for Scratch and Grounder. When Tails is finally caught, Sonic speeds back to save him.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik introduces the Ultra Freeze-O-Matic, announcing his plan to freeze every citizen on Mobius so that he can install Servitude Chips into the frozen citizens and make them unquestionably obedient. Sonic and Tails must foil the scheme.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Robotnik invades Labyrinth, an underwater city, and kidnaps Princess Bubbles, a seahorse-dude named Surff seeks Sonic's help. It seems Surff and the Princess are in love, but the royal family deems Surff unworthy. Sonic, Tails and Surff must avoid the undersea traps and Mobius sea monsters while Sonic rescues the Princess and reunites her with Surff -- making it look like Surff did the whole thing.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik reprograms a machine created by Von Schlemmer capable of turning dreams into reality, unleashing a Nightmare Monster upon Mobius.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When S and G rocket to outer space to mine a radioactive mineral from asteroids, Sonic and Tails stow away. Through a blunder by the robots, all are stranded in space, and Robotnik tries to blow all of them out of the stars. Sonic builds a rocket out of S and G's spare parts, and all return to Mobius.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik's attempt to turn Sonic into an old man with his newest device backfires, turning him and Tails, and later himself, into babies. Now stuck in a day-care, the infantile Sonic and Tails must try to keep Robotnik under control, while Scratch and Grounder attempt to get him back.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik plans to block out the sun and extinguish all life and nature on the planet Mobius. Then he creates a robot son and tries to train him in the ways of wickedness. But when Robotnik Jr. meets Sonic, he becomes a naturalist and teams up with Sonic to thwart Robotnik's evil scheme.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik invents an electron altering generator which makes Scratch and Grounder twice as fast as Sonic. When they lose it and Tails picks it up, Tails speeds through the Mobius countryside leaving disasters and accidents in his wake. Seeing this, Robotnik convinces the villagers that the little fox belongs in jail, and Tails is forced to flee. With Tails all alone, Scratch and Grounder attack, and Sonic makes a last minute rescue.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik captures a Kangaroo named MacHopper, and uses his brain-wipe-reprogramming-computer to transform him into a resourceful demolitions expert (Ala MacGiver). But Mac escapes before Robotnik installs the final Get Sonic program, and teams up with Sonic and Tails to BLAST Robotnik.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic joins up with a group of Dr. Robotnik's rejects who have been banished to the dump and helps them understand that their shortcomings were really their strengths.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When S and G rocket to outer space to mine a radioactive mineral from asteroids, Sonic and Tails stow away. Through a blunder by the robots, all are stranded in space, and Robotnik tries to blow all of them out of the stars. Sonic builds a rocket out of S and G's spare parts, and all return to Mobius.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When a Rebel Base is threatened by Robotnik, Sonic and Tails team up with Big Mike and Da Bears to run a booby-trapped gauntlet in their gigantic Road Warrior-like truck, breaking through every obstacle S and G put in their way until they save the rebels.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik pretends to retire, and lures Sonic and Tails into a phony vacation resort, laden with booby-traps set by Scratch and Grounder. But Sonic takes the bungee jumping, hang gliding, and skydiving in stride, and gives the bashes the bots.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Robotnik and a rival named Quark Rex try to cooperate in capturing Sonic. However, Sonic tricks them into becoming enemies, and they try to outdo each other.

programme.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Coconuts acquires a magic wand and magical gadgets from Wes Weasley (after agreeing to forward all payments to Robotnik), and attempts to master them. Meanwhile, Robotnik tries to change Sonic-themed mobium banknotes into Robotnik banknotes in the Mobius Mint.
